instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface displaying real-time vehicle state data, projected driving paths, and detected object tracks, with data saving and reporting capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ProjectedDrivingPathInsight, ObjectTracksInsight, SaveDataAction, GenerateReportAction

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

instance ACCSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational domain for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems within automotive ADAS"
    base:contains VehicleSystem, HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VedderElectronicSpeedController, SteeringActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands such as enabling/disabling ACC, setting target speed, and emergency braking are generated by the Controlling FE and User Interface FE. These commands are serialized and translated via the Data Translation component, then processed by the Actuation FE which converts desired speed into motor voltage commands for physical actuation. Remote management of ACC is enabled through the UI, with validation occurring during data translation for correct message formatting and unit consistency."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Clock synchronization accuracy requirement of 100 ms for safety-critical vehicular applications due to hardware limitations, aligning with industry standards mandating stricter than 100 ms synchronization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Architectural design based on ISO 23247 RA, with software components mapped to Functional Entities (FEs) implemented as ROS2 packages; User Entity contains 3D Visualization and Control Panel, Digital Twin Entity handles data management and control, Physical Twin Entity manages hardware resources and automotive functions like Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, though Physical Twin Entity's scope deviates from the standard by including general automotive functionality."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin maintains high-fidelity real-time replication through Requirement R7 (maximum network latency of 100 ms) and Requirement R8 (clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms), ensuring precise temporal alignment between physical and digital states."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Intra-component communication uses ROS2 middleware. Inter-entity communication occurs over Wi-Fi with unspecified transport protocol due to truncated documentation. The system must maintain network latency of at most 100 ms per requirement R7. Reliability, redundancy, and security protocols are not detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Entities are deployed across separate hardware components, with vehicle-mounted hardware handling onboard processing and external storage devices managing ego vehicle state data due to limited onboard storage capacity. Deployment is edge-based, with data storage external to the vehicle for scalability and capacity reasons. Specific cloud providers or detailed scalability mechanisms are not mentioned."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Compliance with industry clock synchronization standards requiring 100 ms accuracy for safety-critical vehicular applications, though specific data exchange protocols and external system dependencies are not detailed in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Compliance with ISO 23247 standard, which includes four parts: Overview and general principles, Reference architecture (Part 2), Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, and Information exchange. The standard defines an entity-based reference architecture comprising Observable Manufacturing Elements (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Functional Entities (FEs) within these entities handle specific tasks like data transformation and user interaction, with OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) cited as an example implementation for Data Collection and Device Control Entities."
]
